evidence: Package census: api/ (10 files), config/ (4 files), exploration/ (4 files), indexer/blacklab/ (2 files), model/ (9 files), query/ (4 files), utils/ (3 files), root (Main.java). Total ~37 production files across 8 packages.

model/ holds both semantic-domain types (ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, CoreCollocate, AdjectiveProfile, ComparisonResult, Edge) and query-layer output types (QueryResults). These have different production origins and change drivers.
QueryResults is produced by QueryExecutor (query/ package) and consumed by api/ and exploration/ — its import affinity is with query/ (same package as its producer) not model/.
api/ package has 10 files: 5 HTTP handlers, 1 server, 1 HTTP utility, 1 response builder, 1 renderer (RadialPlot), 1 route wiring. Three distinct concerns share a flat namespace.
RadialPlot generates SVG with spiral layout constants (BASELINE_CANVAS_SIZE, SPIRAL_START_RADIUS, etc.) — a rendering component, not an HTTP adapter.
config/ has 4 cohesive files (GrammarConfig, GrammarConfigLoader, RelationConfig, RelationUtils) — well-sized and focused.
exploration/ has 4 cohesive files (SemanticFieldExplorer, MultiSeedExplorer, CollocateProfileComparator, ExploreOptions) — well-sized.
utils/ has 3 files (CqlUtils, LogDiceCalculator, LongIntHashMap) — cohesive utility package with low coupling.
Main.java at root package is appropriate as the CLI entry point — high fan-in to all subsystems, not a straggler.

strengths: Package names map directly to subsystem responsibilities (exploration/, query/, config/, utils/) — easy to navigate.
Small, cohesive packages: config/ (4), exploration/ (4), query/ (4), utils/ (3) are all appropriately sized.
indexer/blacklab/ uses a sub-package for the BlackLab-specific indexer — correct use of hierarchy.
Main.java at the root is appropriate — it is the entry point with high fan-in, not a straggler.
package_organization.issue_character: Two placement issues both follow the same pattern: a file was added to the most convenient existing package (model/ for QueryResults, api/ for RadialPlot) rather than its ownership-correct home.
package_organization.score_rationale: The package structure is generally clean and navigable; a new engineer can locate any subsystem quickly. The score is held below 85 by two placement issues: QueryResults in model/ (production origin is query/) and RadialPlot in api/ (rendering concern, not HTTP transport). Both affect navigability and change-boundary clarity for future developers. The api/ package at 10 files with three distinct concerns is approaching overload territory but is not yet actionable on its own.

issues.summary: QueryResults lives in model/ but is produced by the query/ layer and has stronger affinity there
evidence: QueryExecutor.java declares all its return types from QueryResults (WordSketchResult, CollocateResult, ConcordanceResult) — QueryResults is functionally part of the query/ API.
model/ package-info.java describes model as 'domain model classes for word-sketch query results and semantic-field exploration' — mixing query output and domain concepts.
Moving QueryResults to query/ would make QueryExecutor and its result types co-located, improving navigability for query-layer changes.
issues.suggestion: Staged reorg: (1) Move QueryResults.java to query/. (2) Update imports in api/ (5 files) and exploration/ (2 files): find src -name '*.java' | xargs grep -l 'QueryResults' | xargs sed -i 's/word_sketch.model.QueryResults/word_sketch.query.QueryResults/g'. (3) Run mvn test to validate. No behavioral change.

issues.summary: RadialPlot (SVG renderer) lives in api/ but has no HTTP-specific logic — breaks the ownership story for the api/ package
evidence: RadialPlot has no import from com.sun.net.httpserver — it is a pure rendering class.
VisualizationHandlers.java is the HTTP adapter: it reads the POST body, calls RadialPlot.renderFromItems(), and sends the SVG response.
A new engineer reading api/ would expect HTTP adapters; encountering a class with SPIRAL_START_RADIUS and SVG generation logic is confusing.
issues.suggestion: Staged reorg: (1) Create api/viz/ sub-package (or a new viz/ top-level package). (2) Move RadialPlot.java there. (3) VisualizationHandlers stays in api/ and imports from api/viz/. (4) mvn test to validate.

root_causes: Files placed in nearest-fit package during feature work rather than ownership-correct package
